comment.body: The Jackson Hole Economic Symposium, hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City, is scheduled for August 21-23, 2025. The event will take place at the Jackson Lake Lodge in Grand Teton National Park, Wyoming. This year's theme is "Labor Markets in Transition: Demographics, Productivity, and Macroeconomic Policy". Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is scheduled to speak on Friday, August 23, at 10:00 a.m. EDT.

comment.body:
This is my main thesis why it's a bear trap.
Fed has kept saying that they are data depending and I don't see them change that tomorrow for their future guidence. 
So, the market knows that.
I don't see him say anything about Trump etc. He is better than that, this is about the future guidence. Not trying to win a battle in the final hours.

I don't think he will mention anything regarding the possibillity about a cut. That is up to the board and not him do decide. 

The market knows already that Fed sees tariffs as inflationary, but it may take some time. The market know this, but sure could interpret this as if we get a cut, there may only be on this year.

Also, the thing is that retail are very bearish into this meeting (specially if one reads on Twitter and watches youtube). 
Big money knows this and I can see them screw retail. Specially since we closed at this bottom range. Anyone who is short here has probably put their stop loss just about the range. 
So at least a fast sweep of the top would be smart to do if the market really wants to go down. 
So, a bear trap if you have a stop loss that is.
Also, If I understand Alma's weekly letter, the market is well positioned through vomma and probably won't break spx 6300
